title: Tuner training cycle
when: The watcher alert 'tuner-cycle-due' fires — either 500+ new training examples accumulated, or 30+ days since the last export.
description: Export training examples and run the tuner model training cycle. This is a human-in-the-loop process: the steward verifies the data, the operator approves the GPU/compute spend, and a delegate_task handles the actual execution. Phase 3 (automated training) is not implemented yet.

steps.label: Check current training data status
steps.command: cd {{COVE_DIR}} && docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'PGUSER=$POSTGRES_USER PGDATABASE=$POSTGRES_DB psql -c "SELECT COUNT(*) AS new_examples_since_export FROM training_examples WHERE created_at > COALESCE((SELECT MAX(exported_at) FROM tuner_exports), '1970-01-01'); SELECT MAX(exported_at) AS last_export FROM tuner_exports;"'
steps.note: Confirm the watcher alert numbers: new examples since last export, and days since last cycle. If the numbers look wrong, investigate before proceeding.

steps.label: Review training examples (sample)
steps.command: cd {{COVE_DIR}} && docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'PGUSER=$POSTGRES_USER PGDATABASE=$POSTGRES_DB psql -c "SELECT id, LEFT(content, 120) AS preview, source, created_at FROM training_examples WHERE created_at > COALESCE((SELECT MAX(exported_at) FROM tuner_exports), '1970-01-01') ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5;"'
steps.note: Spot-check the most recent examples. Look for quality issues, corrupted data, or unexpected sources. Do not export garbage.

steps.label: Export training data to staging
steps.command: cd {{COVE_DIR}} && docker compose exec -T app python -c "import asyncio; from src.utils.watcher import _export_training_data; asyncio.run(_export_training_data())"
steps.note: This creates a JSONL export in the configured export path. Verify the export file exists and has the expected row count. Phase 3 will automate this; for now, run manually or via delegate_task.

steps.label: Verify export integrity
steps.note: Check file size and timestamp. Empty or tiny files mean something went wrong — do not proceed to training.

steps.label: Request GPU/compute allocation
steps.command: # Use the steward queue or delegate_task to request GPU time for training
steps.note: Training requires GPU. The operator must approve compute spend. Do not run training unapproved — this is the human gate per #D44 constraints.

steps.label: Run training (delegate_task)
steps.command: # delegate_task to training agent with the exported data path
steps.note: The actual training execution stays in the steward queue. The training agent handles the GPU work, checkpointing, and model artifact upload. Monitor the task for completion or failure.

steps.label: Record the export cycle
steps.command: cd {{COVE_DIR}} && docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c "PGUSER=$POSTGRES_USER PGDATABASE=$POSTGRES_DB psql -c \"INSERT INTO tuner_exports (example_count, notes) VALUES ((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM training_examples WHERE created_at > COALESCE((SELECT MAX(exported_at) FROM tuner_exports WHERE id != currval('tuner_exports_id_seq')), '1970-01-01')), 'Cycle completed via runbook 07');\""
steps.note: Mark the cycle complete so the watcher alert clears. If training failed, note that in the notes field and keep the alert open for retry.

steps.label: Verify watcher alert cleared
steps.command: cd {{COVE_DIR}} && docker compose exec -T postgres sh -c 'PGUSER=$POSTGRES_USER PGDATABASE=$POSTGRES_DB psql -c "SELECT alert_key, status, title FROM watcher_alerts WHERE category = '''tuner-cycle-due''' AND status = '''open''';"'
steps.note: The tuner-cycle-due alert should auto-resolve after the export is recorded. If it persists, check that the export row was actually inserted.
